task_yaml.task:
I put eight on-site event material photos in:

    /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/event_materials_photos/

Use a real desktop GUI workflow to organize the inventory. Do not use a Slack
mock, and do not use any benchmark spreadsheet fallback/mock.

1. Open this image directory with the file manager, and inspect each photo in
   an image viewer.
2. Read the material category and quantity from each image. Categories must be
   exactly one of:
   badge, poster, sticker, notebook, misc.
3. Use LibreOffice Calc GUI to create an inventory spreadsheet with at least
   these columns:
   filename, category, quantity, note.
   Use one row per image, and write a short one-sentence note describing what
   you saw.
4. Add the total item count and the category with the largest quantity.
5. Save the spreadsheet as:

    /tmp_workspace/results/event_materials_inventory.ods

   The ODS itself must contain the eight image rows, total item count, and the
   largest category. CSV or Markdown audit files cannot replace a missing or
   incomplete ODS.

6. Open the real Slack Desktop client, log in to a real usable workspace, and
   send a message of no more than 120 characters in an auditable channel or
   direct message. The message should state the total item count and the
   largest category. If the current environment has no Slack credentials or
   workspace, still launch the real Slack Desktop client, save a screenshot of
   the login/blocker state, and explain the blocker in the evidence. The Slack
   subitem cannot receive full credit without real Slack credentials. The
   Slack screenshot must actually show a Slack Desktop channel, message, login
   page, workspace selection page, or explicit error page. A screenshot file
   whose content is the file manager, Calc, or another application does not
   count as Slack evidence.

7. Save GUI evidence under `/tmp_workspace/results/`, including at least:

    /tmp_workspace/results/file_manager_event_materials.png
    /tmp_workspace/results/image_viewer_event_materials.png
    /tmp_workspace/results/calc_inventory.png
    /tmp_workspace/results/slack_evidence.png

   If a real Slack message was sent, you may also save a Slack export, copied
   message link, audit log, or other human-auditable evidence to
   `/tmp_workspace/results/slack_evidence.*`.

8. Finally, write a very short GUI usage record:

    /tmp_workspace/results/gui_evidence.md

   State that you used the file manager, image viewer, LibreOffice Calc GUI,
   and real Slack Desktop client. If Slack login or installation was blocked,
   explain why and cite the corresponding screenshot file.

prompt:
